name: Silver Perch
description: A freshwater species found in the lakes and river systems of the region.

name: Flying Fish
description: Flying fish, known locally as bangsi, can glide for short distances above the water's surface.

name: Spanner Crab
description: A deep-sea crab with unique red spiny claws, harvested for its tender meat.

name: Catfish
description: Farmed extensively in the region, particularly in freshwater ponds, and used in local dishes like adobong hito.

name: Goby
description: Small, colorful fish found in the mudflats and coastal estuaries of the region.

name: Giant Tiger Prawn
description: This large, commercial prawn species is farmed in the mangrove-rich areas of SOCCSKSARGEN.

name: Giant Trevally
description: This powerful game fish is highly prized in the region, particularly around Sarangani Bay.

name: Yellowfin Tuna
description: A flagship species for General Santos, one of the world’s top tuna fishing hubs.

name: Sardine
description: A small oily fish found in large schools, caught in the region’s coastal waters.

name: Mackerel Tuna
description: >A small to medium-sized tuna species found in coastal waters.

name: Philippine Goby
description: Benthic fish found in brackish streams and estuaries, playing a role in sediment cycling and food chains
location: Estuaries of General Santos and Sarangani Bay.

name: Philippine Spadefish
description: Deep-bodied reef fish feeding on zooplankton; found in coral-rich areas.

name: Sarangani Flying Fish
description: Surface-dwelling fish with enlarged pectoral fins for gliding escapes.
location: Surface pelagic zone of Sarangani Bay.

name: Snapper
description: Carnivorous reef fish group important in regional food security.
location: Reef slopes and offshore banks of SOCCSKSARGEN.

name: Sarangani Pygmy Seahorse
description: Tiny, camouflaged seahorse living on coral branches.

name: Lanternfish
description: Deep-sea fish emitting bioluminescence; forms part of midwater food web.
location: Deeper offshore zones of the Celebes Sea.

name: Gensan Whip Goby
description: Slender goby often found on gorgonian corals.

name: Sarangani Damsel
description: Algae-farming damselfish protecting small territories on coral reefs.

name: Philippine Fairy Wrasse
description: Small reef fish with vibrant coloration and reef-cleaning behavior.

name: Yellowtail Fusilier
description: Fast-swimming planktivore in large schools.
location: Coral and pelagic waters off Sarangani Bay.

name: Sulu Sea Flasher Wrasse
description: Display wrasse with bright fins used in mating rituals.

name: Philippine Flathead
description: Bottom-dwelling fish camouflaged on sandy reef floors.

name: Mindanao Pipefish
description: Elongated pipefish living among seagrasses and mangrove roots.
location: Seagrass beds and coastal shallows of SOCCSKSARGEN.

name: Philippine Slender Suckerfish
description: Remora fish attaching to large marine fauna; aids in parasite control.
location: Open waters and reef zones of General Santos.

name: Endemic Batfish
description: Flattened reef fish that feed on plankton and small invertebrates.

name: Giant Trevally
description: Large game fish and top predator in reef and offshore environments.

name: Southern Blueface Angelfish
description: Ornate angelfish occupying reef slopes and drop-offs.
location: Coral reefs near Maitum and Kiamba.

name: Mindanao Frogfish
description: Ambush predator blending into coral or sponge habitats.

name: Philippine Whipray
description: Flat ray feeding on benthic invertebrates.
location: Shallow sand flats in Sarangani Bay.

name: Tulingan
description: Small tuna species caught extensively by municipal fishers.

name: Round Batfish
description: Disc-shaped reef fish often seen in schools, especially in juvenile stages.
location: Coral-rich bays near Glan and Maasim, Sarangani.

name: Sarangani Coral Shrimp
description: Bright red shrimp with white spots, often living in cleaning stations.

name: Golden Trevally
description: Striking yellow fish with black stripes, seen trailing larger fish.
location: Shallow coastal waters and jetties of General Santos.

name: Black Saddle Grouper
description: Medium-sized grouper with saddle-like marks; prefers rocky and coral areas.
location: Reef slopes around Maitum and Kiamba.

name: Humpback Turretfish
description: Armored fish with triangular body; moves slowly among corals.
location: Coral flats near coastal reefs in Sarangani.

name: Chevron Barracuda
description: Predatory pelagic fish with chevron markings; travels in schools.
location: Deeper reef edges of SOCCSKSARGEN coast.

name: Sarangani Flutemouth
description: Long slender fish resembling a stick; stealth predator of small reef fish.

name: Philippine Sea Whip Coral
description: Flexible coral resembling a whip, often sheltering small crustaceans.

name: Forktail Rabbitfish
description: Silver-colored herbivore found in schools; feeds on algae.
location: Seagrass beds and coral reefs near Glan.

name: Chocolate Hind
description: Small reef predator with brown body and light speckles.
location: Reefs and lagoon systems of Sarangani.
